reasoning: This question resolves positively if a peer-reviewed paper claims evidence of lepton universality violation at the 5-sigma level before the end of 2025. The initial basis for this question—a 3.1-sigma anomaly found by the LHCb collaboration in 2021—was resolved when updated measurements from LHCb in December 2022 restored agreement with lepton flavor universality and the Standard Model. This "flatlining" of the anomaly essentially eliminated the original prospect of a 5-sigma violation. Furthermore, the community prediction on Metaculus dropped to 1% by October 2025, which underscores that no surprise experiments found a 5-sigma violation in the interim. Since physics anomalies of this type often take years of data collection to solidify, the absence of any leading anomalies in 2024–2025 makes a sudden 5-sigma discovery before 2026 vanishingly unlikely. (0.02 is the lowest permissible prediction in this framework).
